chunks.text: In spring, the cherry blossoms began to open.
Last winter was especially harsh, and many people were anxious, waiting for the blossoms.
Cherry blossoms are a special flower for the Japanese.
Since ancient times, they have been written into poems, drawn in paintings
and have become a feature of the culture.
The custom of holding a flower - viewing party under the cherry trees has a history of more than a thousand years.
When we see the cherry trees in full bloom, everyone swallows their breath.
The sight of petals dancing in the wind is astonishing in its beauty.
But the beauty of the cherry blossom lies in its brevity.
In about a week, the flowers fall.
The petals pile on the ground like a carpet.
That scene, too, gives off a unique beauty.
In Japanese culture
cherry blossoms represent " mono no aware " — a quiet sadness and love toward that which inevitably fades.
Because the time of bloom is short, it feels all the more beautiful.
Life, too, is as fleeting as cherry blossoms.
A decade, looking back, passes in an instant.
Youth, health, time with loved ones — none of it will last forever.
That is why we should cherish this moment, right now.
Under the cherry trees, to speak with friends, laugh with family
and quietly look up at the sky alone.
The sum of such small moments is life itself.
The cherry trees bloomed again this year.
Next year, and the year after
I want to see as many cherry blossoms as I can.
That simple wish is what keeps us alive.
